theme: Size pipes for quadratic pressure loss
title: Reservoir Trunk Main: Sizing Pipes Against Quadratic Head Loss
strategies: uniform baseline: give every pipe the same diameter, the largest whose network-wide uniform cost fits the budget (reproduces the checker's own baseline)
linear demand-share greedy: water-fill the budget proportional to each edge's own downstream flow Q_e (a plausible but LINEAR exchange rate)

exact marginal-value greedy: repeatedly buy the single diameter upgrade with the best true (unmet-demand reduction)/(marginal cost), evaluated exactly via the subtree-wide impact of each edge's head loss -- exploits the Q^2/D^5 convexity and downstream compounding that the linear recipe misses
invalid: out-of-range diameter indices, rejected by feasibility validation
